opcode.name: OP_MOTD
opcode.comment: Server message of the day. Test: 303b S>C plaintext in world stream, fires after OP_EnterWorld (not at zone-in despite client-side rendering at that point). Text not template-keyed — full sysadmin-typed message body inline. Skipped on world-handshake cycles that don't actually proceed to EnterWorld (e.g. fresh-from-CharCreate relogin in 2026-05-15 capture).

opcode.name: OP_LoginServerInfo
opcode.comment: Login-server→client server-list entry (one per available world). Test: 219b S>C login-stage. Body shape: leading u32 + 8 bytes + zstring hostname (eqworldNN.everquest.com) + 8 bytes + u32 length-of-locale-block + u32 count + null-separated locale strings (e.g. "Test\0EN\0US\0Standard (Live Environment)\0<disclaimer>\0"). Daemon does not currently dispatch this packet at app-op level — see TEST_OPCODE.md 2026-05-05 entry.

opcode.name: OP_SendLoginInfo
opcode.comment: Client starting world session by asking for login information

opcode.name: OP_EnterWorld
opcode.comment: Server approval for client to enter world

opcode.name: OP_ExpansionInfo
opcode.comment: Which expansions user has. Test: 64b fixed S>C in the post-LogServer / pre-ApproveWorld world handshake band. Header u32 = expansion-count (= 8 observed), then bitmask + per-login session ID at offset 36, then trailer with `0x07 0x02` tier markers. Per-login fires with mostly-static content.

opcode.name: OP_SendCharInfo
opcode.comment: Send all chars visible @ char select

opcode.name: OP_SendSpellChecksum
opcode.comment: Contains a snippet of spell data. 2056b C>S, 1st in fire order on world handshake (revised from 0x2de1). Also re-fires on zone change (spell DB integrity check).

opcode.name: OP_SendExeChecksum
opcode.comment: 4th in fire order on world handshake. 64b C>S, payload `04 00 00 00` + 60-byte hash.

opcode.name: OP_SendBaseDataChecksum
opcode.comment: 2nd in fire order on world handshake. 2056b C>S (revised from 0x289c). Only fires on initial full handshake (not on zone change).

opcode.name: OP_SendSkillCapsChecksum
opcode.comment: 3rd in fire order on world handshake. 2056b C>S (revised from 0xa958). Only fires on initial full handshake (not on zone change).

opcode.name: OP_SetChatServer
opcode.comment: Chat server connection: 56b S>C carrying the legacy comma-separated tuple `host,port,server.charname,sessionkey,N` (e.g. lvseq-chat01.everquest.com,9879,test.Charname,<key>,0) — the original IP/Port/servername.charname/password format Test still uses.

opcode.name: OP_SetChatServer2
opcode.comment: Secondary chat / account / commerce service catalog: 1464b S>C, ~18 plaintext Daybreak service URLs (auth, account, commerce, support, premium). Replaces the legacy second-chat-server tuple with a multi-URL service catalog on Test.

opcode.name: OP_ZoneServerInfo
opcode.comment: Zone server connection info: 130b S>C with the zone server hostname (e.g. eqzone-31.everquest.com) at offset 0 and port (u16 at offset 128). Fires after the zone-handoff world handshake to point the client at the next zone server.

opcode.name: OP_WorldComplete
opcode.comment: Client telling world server it is done. World replies by disconnecting.

opcode.name: OP_DeleteCharacter
opcode.comment: Delete character @ char select. Test: 68b C>S, NUL-terminated char name @ offset 0 in a char[64] slot (post-name bytes are uninitialized client-stack leak), trailer u32 @ offset 64.

opcode.name: OP_CharacterCreate
opcode.comment: Create character @ char select. Test: 168b C>S, race@0x44 / class@0x48 / deity@0x4c / starting zoneId@0x50 (name already reserved via prior OP_ApproveName).

opcode.name: OP_ApproveName
opcode.comment: Approving new character name @ char creation. Test: 84b C>S name candidate (name@0 in char[64], race@64, class@68, deity@72), 1b S>C status (0x12 = available, 0x01 = committed).
